description: Seed targets for official-doc collection. Guardrails are intentionally strict to avoid near-match framework docs.

targets.name: AWS Amplify Gen 2 for Next.js
targets.scope_note: Collect only the Next.js documentation surface. Reject adjacent React or JavaScript pages that do not include the Next.js designation.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when the project needs a fast App Router foundation with AWS-managed auth, data, storage, or function layers that can start simple and scale cleanly.
cloudraven_examples: Prototype a client portal with email sign-in, protected routes, and scoped file storage.
Stand up a campaign operations workspace where app data and asset storage share one managed backend surface.
applicability_review: High fit when CloudRaven wants one repo to express both frontend and backend intent.
Review access rules early so prototype shortcuts do not leak into production authorization.

targets.name: Next.js App Router
targets.scope_note: Collect official App Router, route handler, and data-fetching guidance for the runtime this repo uses.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material for the server-first application shell, route handlers, and data-fetching model that frame the public site and authenticated workspace.
cloudraven_examples: Keep the account shell server-bootstrapped while hydrating client-heavy operator panels only where interaction requires it.
Use route handlers for provider callbacks, notification webhooks, and workspace-side integrations.
applicability_review: High fit because the repo already depends on App Router boundaries, server data bootstrapping, and route handlers.
Cache and revalidation behavior should be reviewed before shipping new data-heavy or integration-heavy surfaces.

targets.name: AWS SDK for JavaScript v3
targets.scope_note: Collect the modular SDK developer guide relevant to direct service-client usage inside handlers and shared runtimes.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when Amplify handlers need direct access to AWS service clients for orchestration, mail, secrets, storage, or persistence operations.
cloudraven_examples: Call Step Functions directly from custom mutations that launch multi-step workflows.
Use Secrets Manager, SES, S3, and DynamoDB clients from shared runtime helpers without hiding the operational contracts.
applicability_review: High fit because the repo directly imports modular AWS SDK clients in multiple handlers.
Client reuse, error handling, retries, and IAM scope should be reviewed as first-class implementation concerns.

targets.name: AWS CDK v2
targets.scope_note: Collect the CDK v2 guide material most relevant to backend assembly, IAM, and Step Functions wiring.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when `amplify/backend.ts` needs deeper infra customization than the default Amplify resource wrappers provide.
cloudraven_examples: Attach additional IAM policies or event-source wiring to Amplify-defined resources.
Assemble Step Functions, environment wiring, and cross-resource permissions in a single backend composition layer.
applicability_review: High fit because the repo's deployment source of truth includes meaningful CDK customization.
Keep higher-level architecture docs in sync whenever backend assembly patterns change.

targets.name: AWS SES Email Receiving
targets.scope_note: Collect the SES email receiving, S3 delivery, permission, and receipt-rule docs that matter for inbound mail workflows.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when the repo manages inbound email receiving with SES, S3 delivery, rule activation, and workflow triggers.
cloudraven_examples: Route workspace-agent public aliases through SES receipt rules into an S3-backed inbound workflow.
Troubleshoot receipt-rule deployment failures where SES validates S3 delivery permissions before the full stack is stable.
applicability_review: High fit because workspace-agent ingress depends on SES receipt rules and S3 delivery, not just mailbox polling.
Review permission shape, region support, and deployment ordering before treating SES receiving as routine infrastructure.

targets.name: OpenAI API
targets.scope_note: Collect API docs and guides only.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when the project needs API-first intelligence layers for research, generation, orchestration, or structured outputs.
cloudraven_examples: Build a task researcher that produces structured outputs for downstream automation.
Add a knowledge assistant that can summarize project artifacts and propose next actions.
applicability_review: High fit for programmable AI workflows that must live inside a custom app or local toolchain.
Always verify model and tool guidance in the current official docs before implementation.

targets.name: ElevenLabs API
targets.scope_note: Collect voice and API entry points only.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when a CloudRaven workflow needs speech output, narrated artifacts, or voice-first assistance.
cloudraven_examples: Generate narrated summaries for marketing or knowledge artifacts.
Prototype a voice-enabled support or intake experience.
applicability_review: Best fit when voice adds utility, not just novelty.
Review permissions, latency, and transcript handling before production use.

targets.name: HeroUI React
targets.scope_note: Collect the official HeroUI React LLM exports for guides, components, and patterns.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when the app needs fast interface assembly with a coherent component system.
cloudraven_examples: Assemble internal dashboards, onboarding flows, and admin surfaces quickly.
Pair with Tailwind when the app needs both structured components and custom layout work.
applicability_review: High fit for operational interfaces and starter portals.
Define theming choices early if the product needs strong branding.

targets.name: shadcn/ui
targets.scope_note: Collect the core shadcn/ui docs for installation, CLI usage, configuration, theming, and the LLM-readable index.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when the app wants an open-code component system that can be installed, owned, and customized directly inside the repo.
cloudraven_examples: Replace package-based UI dependencies with copied-in components the team can edit freely.
Build dashboards, forms, and admin surfaces with Tailwind-aligned components that stay close to the app code.
applicability_review: High fit when the team wants design-system control without depending on a closed component package abstraction.
Review component ownership, update strategy, and styling conventions early so copied-in code does not drift inconsistently.

targets.name: Google Identity and Workspace Events
targets.scope_note: Collect social sign-in plus Gmail and Calendar push event guidance.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material for consumer sign-in and Google Workspace-driven notification workflows.
cloudraven_examples: Link Google sign-in with inbox listeners to automate lead or support flows.
Use calendar push channels to trigger follow-up actions around scheduling changes.
applicability_review: Strong fit for assistant workflows that need mailbox or calendar awareness.
Watch channel renewal and scope management must be part of the design.

targets.name: Microsoft Entra ID and Graph Change Notifications
targets.scope_note: Collect Microsoft identity platform and Graph change notification guidance.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material for enterprise authentication and Microsoft 365 event-driven workflows.
cloudraven_examples: Authenticate workforce users with Microsoft identity and process Outlook mailbox updates.
Listen for calendar changes to update project workflows or coordination automations.
applicability_review: High fit for B2B and enterprise collaboration products.
Tenant type, consent model, and subscription lifecycle need early design attention.

targets.name: Pexels API
targets.scope_note: Collect official API documentation only.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material for licensed stock media retrieval, not image generation.
cloudraven_examples: Populate decks, landing pages, or campaign drafts with stock imagery quickly.
Source placeholder visuals for prototypes while preserving attribution context.
applicability_review: Good fit when branded generated imagery is not required.
Track attribution and licensing context with stored assets.

targets.name: HubSpot Developer Platform
targets.scope_note: Collect OAuth and transactional email guidance relevant to the repo's mailbox and delivery workflows.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material for HubSpot mailbox connection, delivery staging, and transactional outbound workflows.
cloudraven_examples: Connect a workspace mailbox owner to HubSpot and stage approved drafts into a transactional send flow.
Keep provider-specific delivery requirements aligned with workspace approval and audit events.
applicability_review: High fit because the repo already includes HubSpot OAuth and outbound delivery handlers.
Review portal ownership, token lifecycle, and provider-specific send limits before expanding usage.

targets.name: Tailwind CSS
targets.scope_note: Collect the Next.js setup path and core utility styling guidance.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when fast layout iteration and custom interface composition matter.
cloudraven_examples: Build a branded portal shell with responsive utility classes.
Layer custom presentation or dashboard layouts over a component system.
applicability_review: High fit for rapid iteration and unusual UI compositions.
Create conventions early so utility usage does not become unstructured.

targets.name: Iconify React
targets.scope_note: Collect the React component usage guidance for the icon layer used across the app UI.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when the repo needs consistent icon composition across marketing and workspace interfaces.
cloudraven_examples: Keep section navigation, status indicators, and callout blocks visually consistent without introducing a second icon package.
Choose icon loading patterns that avoid unnecessary payload growth on dense UI surfaces.
applicability_review: High fit because Iconify is used widely across the app shell and public site.
Review icon selection and bundle footprint when expanding UI-heavy surfaces.

targets.name: MapLibre GL JS
targets.scope_note: Collect API entry points and examples for interactive map work.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material for open, portable map-based interfaces and geospatial storytelling.
cloudraven_examples: Visualize service coverage, assets, or route activity on a live map.
Create location-aware dashboards without locking into a proprietary front-end map SDK.
applicability_review: Strong fit when the map stack should stay open and customizable.
Choose tile, geocoding, and style providers deliberately.

targets.name: react-map-gl
targets.scope_note: Collect the React wrapper guidance used to host map surfaces in the app.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when React panels need a controlled map wrapper over the MapLibre runtime.
cloudraven_examples: Embed project geospatial context inside workspace cards without hand-rolling a raw map lifecycle wrapper.
Coordinate map state with React panel state while keeping the underlying map stack portable.
applicability_review: High fit where map interaction lives inside React-driven workspace panels.
Check controlled-state and event-binding patterns before adding more complex geospatial UI.

targets.name: Apache ECharts
targets.scope_note: Collect the starter handbook for charting work.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material for dashboards and data storytelling views.
cloudraven_examples: Render operational metrics dashboards for internal teams.
Turn collected activity data into chart-driven reports.
applicability_review: Best fit when charts directly support user decisions.
Plan responsive behavior and export needs early.

targets.name: Three.js
targets.scope_note: Collect docs and starter manual pages for 3D web work.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when 3D interaction meaningfully improves comprehension, product exploration, or storytelling.
cloudraven_examples: Visualize a physical product or spatial layout in a browser-based experience.
Create interactive scenes for higher-impact demos or presentations.
applicability_review: High fit for product visualization and experiential demos.
Reserve it for tasks that genuinely benefit from 3D.

targets.name: PptxGenJS
targets.scope_note: Collect the official presentation-generation documentation for deck automation workflows.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material for editable slide, print, and artifact generation from repo-local source data.
cloudraven_examples: Generate sales, sprint, or case-study decks from structured content and reusable layout helpers.
Keep artifact-as-code workflows editable instead of exporting flattened images too early.
applicability_review: High fit because the repo already contains active deck-generation workflows under `docs/slides/`.
Treat typography, image sizing, and text-layout helpers as part of the system contract, not incidental implementation details.

targets.name: Sharp Image Processing
targets.scope_note: Collect the official image-processing guidance relevant to printable and campaign-artifact generation.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material when repo-generated print or marketing artifacts need deterministic image composition.
cloudraven_examples: Compose branded print assets from artwork, text layers, and generated QR codes.
Resize or export campaign assets into multiple formats without leaving the repo workflow.
applicability_review: Good fit for deterministic artifact generation that should stay scriptable and local.
Validate color, density, and export settings when outputs are meant for print or client-facing collateral.

targets.name: Stripe and EventBridge
targets.scope_note: Collect billing and AWS event-destination entry points.
targets.cloudraven_focus: Use this material for payments, subscriptions, and event-driven back-office workflows.
cloudraven_examples: Connect subscription events to AWS-native processing and entitlement updates.
Prototype monetization flows with hosted checkout before deeper custom billing work.
applicability_review: Strong fit for monetization experiments and production-ready payment flows.
Keep billing state and event processing rules aligned from the start.
